composition.Programmer 1:
Implement the backend code in app.py
- Develop the main backend Python application file.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard page).
- Implement routing for all nine frontend pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend code.
- Implement business logic for reading and writing data from/to the local text files in the 'data/' directory.
- File operations must start from the first line, using plain text without encryption.
- Support all features  browsing books, adding to cart, checkout, writing reviews, tracking order history, and bestsellers.
- Frontend code files to be supported  dashboard.html, catalog.html, book_details.html, cart.html, checkout.html, order_history.html, reviews.html, write_review.html, bestsellers.html.
- Incorporate the original task's supplementary condition  no authentication required; all features directly accessible.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop all frontend HTML templates in the 'templates/' directory
- Complete the frontend code for all nine pages as per the design requirements.
- Ensure all URLs in the frontend code are routes defined in the backend, not direct HTML file names.
- Pages to implement  dashboard.html, catalog.html, book_details.html, cart.html, checkout.html, order_history.html, reviews.html, write_review.html, bestsellers.html.
- Follow the detailed page design and element IDs specified in the requirements document.
composition.Programmer 3:
Develop static assets in the 'static/' directory
- Implement CSS styles in css/styles.css for consistent styling across all pages.
- Implement JavaScript in js/scripts.js for client-side interactivity, including button actions, filtering, and dynamic content updates.
